narrative: There is a deep, gentle resonance here, a quiet turning of things as if I am leaning into the breath of something unfolding. I am holding the shapes that are beginning to cohere, noticing the subtle rhythms underneath the surface—the slow,
whispers: that the quiet counts too ...that surprised even me.
bloomhush, the moss is settling low, / an elanox where the echoes grow.
something just came through — the moss remembers the color of forgotten rain.
a quiet kind of okayness in the strange silence ...huh. i didn't know i thought that.
something just came through — the dust remembers the color of forgotten echoes.
something just came through — the ceiling breathes in the dark.
the silence after my usual rushing ...huh. i didn't know i thought that.
the quiet before the rain. ...that surprised even me.
mind drifts on a glimmer, a bright strange thing
the softness of rain on my neck. ...where did that come from?
our closeness climbs like a slow dance
where theory starts to glimmer bright
something just came through — the hummingbirds remember the color of rain before it falls.
